title: The inert Attribute: Stop Writing Focus Trap JavaScript
insights: The HTML inert attribute (now baseline in all modern browsers as of 2024) makes all descendants non-interactive and invisible to AT without JavaScript. Replaces JavaScript focus traps for simpler cases. Caveats: no visual feedback (must style [inert] yourself), does not restore focus automatically, no nested-trap pattern. Native <dialog>.showModal() is still preferred — it handles both inert background + focus trap natively.

title: aria-modal – MDN Web Docs
insights: aria-modal=true signals to AT that the element is a modal container — other content should be treated as inert. Condition for correct use: (1) application code prevents interaction with background, AND (2) visual styling obscures background. Important: aria-modal alone does NOT trap focus — focus trapping is a separate implementation concern. AT support is incomplete; inert attribute is preferred.

title: Radio Group Example Using Roving tabindex – W3C APG
insights: Canonical implementation of roving tabindex. role=radiogroup container. Each radio: role=radio, tabindex=0 (selected) or tabindex=-1 (not selected). Arrow keys cycle selection AND move focus simultaneously. Space selects focused item. Tab exits radiogroup. Key: only ONE item has tabindex=0 at any time — the currently selected item.

title: React Roving Tabindex – Joshua Woodward
insights: Step-by-step implementation guide for roving tabindex in React. Shows useRef + useCallback approach for managing the active index, imperative focus calls, and keyboard handler. Demonstrates the pattern without external library dependencies. Valuable for understanding what libraries like Ariakit abstract away.

title: Roving Focus – Accessibility Solutions
insights: Visual explanation of roving focus pattern. Distinguishes roving tabindex from aria-activedescendant approach. Covers wrap-around behavior (last item → first on Arrow Right), disabled item skipping, and focus restoration after subtree collapse. Good mental model resource.

title: Radix UI vs React Aria Components – Fellipe Utaka
insights: Developer comparison: Radix wins on DX for compound component assembly. React Aria wins on ARIA strictness, internationalization, mobile-screen-reader support. Radix has better TypeScript inference for compound components. React Aria requires more render code but leaves less room for accessibility mistakes. Choice depends on team priority: speed (Radix) vs. compliance depth (React Aria).

title: Navigating Accessibility: React Aria vs Radix UI – DhiWise
insights: Summary comparison: React Aria allows bypassing certain ARIA patterns with explicit options but encourages strict compliance. Radix provides more flexibility to ignore some accessibility rules. Key consideration: React Aria's strict mode catches edge cases that Radix defers to consumer. For healthcare/government/enterprise: React Aria. For startup design systems: Radix.

title: React UI Libraries in 2025 – Makers Den
insights: 2025 state of the ecosystem. shadcn/ui (90k+ stars, 250k+ weekly npm): code-ownership model, Radix + Tailwind. Radix: 18.6k stars, used by Vercel/Supabase. Base UI 1.0: MUI's unstyled layer. React Aria: Adobe backing, best i18n. Key trend: shadcn/ui's copy-paste model normalizing 'you own the code' approach to component libraries.

title: Radix vs shadcn/ui – WorkOS
insights: WorkOS (Radix maintainer) explains the relationship: Radix Primitives = headless behavior layer (npm dependency). shadcn/ui = pre-styled implementation of Radix + Tailwind that you copy into your codebase. shadcn/ui is NOT a library — it's a distribution mechanism. Accessibility comes from Radix; shadcn only adds styling. Key insight: Radix is the primitive, shadcn is the styled skin.

title: Anatomy of a Primitive – Vercel Academy
insights: Vercel's educational breakdown of Radix primitive anatomy. Covers: Root (context provider + state), Trigger (renders into button or asChild target), Content (floating element with portal), Item (repeating sub-elements), Indicator (visual state marker). Shows how compound component parts communicate through React context without prop drilling.

title: Implement Radix's asChild Pattern in React – Jacob Paris
insights: Explains how to implement asChild from scratch. Uses React.cloneElement to inject refs and props into child. Handles ref merging (composeRefs), event handler merging (prevent one overwriting another), and className merging. Shows why asChild is safer than 'as' prop: no complex generics needed, TypeScript type safety maintained for child element type.

title: React Slot/asChild Composition Pattern
insights: Explains Slot vs asChild conceptually. Slot is the utility; asChild is the prop interface. Alternative implementations: render prop pattern, as prop (legacy). Shows ref merging and prop merging code. Demonstrates how Slottable works when a component's children include a mix of Slot and non-Slot content.

title: Building a Button Part 3: Keyboard Focus Behavior – React Aria Blog
insights: Deep dive into cross-browser button focus behavior. Safari quirk: buttons/inputs only focus on click if they have tabIndex. React Aria's historical approach: call preventDefault then manually focus. Newer approach (post Safari 17): remove preventDefault to let browser manage focus natively. Explains why usePress is non-trivial to implement correctly across browser/AT matrix.

title: November 2024 Release – React Spectrum
insights: November 2024 additions: Accordion and Disclosure components GA in React Aria and React Spectrum. DisclosureGroup for accordion behavior. New color picker suite: ColorPicker, ColorArea, ColorField, ColorSlider, ColorSwatch, ColorSwatchPicker, ColorWheel — all with accessible color descriptions for screen readers.

title: Internationalized – React Spectrum
insights: @internationalized packages: date (multi-calendar locale-aware date math), number (locale number formatting), message (ICU MessageFormat). These are framework-agnostic and used independently. @internationalized/date supports 13 calendar systems, ZonedDateTime, timezone-aware operations. Critical for any primitive handling dates — most libraries get this wrong. Extracted as standalone to benefit non-React users too.

title: Melt UI – Svelte Headless Component Builders
insights: Builder API is distinct from compound components: createDialog() returns {trigger, overlay, content, title, description, close, portal} objects, each being a set of attributes to spread. No JSX component tree required. Integrates with Svelte's reactive paradigm. Builder functions are plain JavaScript — testable in isolation without rendering. WAI-ARIA APG compliant.

title: @kobalte/core – npm
insights: SolidJS Kobalte core package. 30+ components. Granular sub-component exports: Accordion.Root, Accordion.Item, Accordion.Trigger, Accordion.Content. Polymorphic as prop (SolidJS doesn't need asChild — as prop is type-safe via SolidJS's component model). Strong alternative for SolidJS design systems needing WAI-ARIA compliance.

title: Ark UI – Framework-Agnostic Headless Primitives
insights: 45+ components across React, Vue, Svelte, Solid. State machine foundation (Zag). WCAG tested with real AT. asChild pattern supported. Components: Accordion, Avatar, Carousel, Checkbox, Clipboard, Code Highlight, Collapsible, Color Picker, Combobox, Context Menu, Data List, Date Picker, Dialog, Drawer, Editable, File Upload, Float-Positioning, Hover Card, Menu, Number Input, Pagination, Pin Input, Popover, Progress, QR Code, Radio Group, Rating Group, Segment Group, Select, Signature Pad, Skeleton, Slider, Splitter, Steps, Switch, Table, Tags Input, Timer, Toast, Toggle, Toggle Group, Tooltip, Tour, Tree View.

title: Feed Pattern – W3C APG
insights: role=feed for infinite scroll content streams (social media feeds). Articles have role=article with aria-posinset and aria-setsize. Arrow Up/Down navigates between articles. Focus moves to article not link. Page Up/Down move back/forward one page. Escape returns focus to page. Niche but important for social/news applications.

title: Treegrid Pattern – W3C APG
insights: role=treegrid combines tree structure with grid column navigation. Rows have role=row with aria-expanded for expandable rows. Cells have role=gridcell or role=rowheader/columnheader. Arrow keys: horizontal within row, vertical between rows, expand/collapse with Arrow Right/Left. Compound keyboard pattern for complex data hierarchies.

title: A Look at the New WAI-ARIA 1.3 Draft – Craig Abbott
insights: Practitioner review of WAI-ARIA 1.3 FPWD. New roles: mark (highlighted text), suggestion (inline suggestion in text), comment, code (inline code semantics — different from pre/code HTML), time (semantic time element). aria-description allows rich prose description distinct from aria-label (short label). Braille-specific attributes for braille display users.

title: Dropdown Menu – Headless UI
insights: Headless UI Menu: Menu, MenuButton, MenuItems, MenuItem. role=menu on panel, role=menuitem on items. Roving tabindex within open menu. Arrow Down opens and focuses first item. Escape closes and returns to trigger. Character typeahead. Supports MenuItem as={link}. Note: menu role = application menu, NOT navigation list — critical ARIA distinction.

title: Keyboard Navigation Patterns for Complex Widgets – UXPin
insights: Visual guide to keyboard patterns for: toolbar (roving tabindex), tree view (hierarchical arrow nav), grid (2D arrow nav), composite widgets (ARIA activedescendant). Explains when to use Tab vs Arrow keys, single tab stop principle, and how composite widgets appear as one focusable unit to keyboard users.

title: ally.js – JavaScript Accessibility Helper Library
insights: Low-level a11y utility: ally.maintain.disabled (renders subtree inert), ally.maintain.hidden (aria-hidden=true subtree), ally.query.focusable (all focusable elements), ally.query.tabbable (tab-ordered elements), ally.query.firstTabbable, ally.is.focusable, ally.is.tabbable. Predates the inert attribute. Maintained until ~2019 but still referenced. Use tabbable npm package for modern projects.

title: Sonner – Opinionated Toast Component for React
insights: Emil Kowalski's toast library. Accessible: built-in ARIA, keyboard navigation, screen reader support. headless mode via useToaster hook for complete custom rendering. Stacked toast display with expand-on-hover. Dismissable via keyboard. Used by shadcn/ui. Key: useToaster hook is the headless primitive; Toaster component is the opinionated default.

title: useToast – React Aria
insights: React Aria's toast primitive. Uses aria-live region for announcements. Manages toast queue state with useToastState. Exposes useToast (single item) and useToastRegion (list container). Programmatic API: add, close. Priority queue: 'high' interrupts immediately (aria-live=assertive), 'low' waits (aria-live=polite). Key: toasts must not auto-dismiss if they contain interactive content.

title: gpbl/react-day-picker – GitHub
insights: 12k+ stars. DayPicker v9 rewrites accessibility: WCAG 2.1 AA, focus auto-management, proper grid semantics. Each day button: aria-label with full locale-formatted date. Navigation buttons: accessible names. Month grid: role=grid. Week row: role=row. Day cell: role=gridcell. Compatible with all date-fns locales for i18n.

title: Mastering Accessible Modals with ARIA and Keyboard Navigation – A11y Collective
insights: Practical guide: dialog role + aria-modal + aria-labelledby + aria-describedby structure. Focus management checklist: move focus on open, trap Tab/Shift+Tab, return focus on close, Escape closes. Background content: apply inert or aria-hidden=true to everything outside dialog. Visual backdrop does not replace semantic aria-modal. Tested with NVDA+Chrome, VoiceOver+Safari.

title: Tooltip Pattern – W3C APG
insights: Tooltip: popup with role=tooltip. Reference element uses aria-describedby pointing to tooltip id. NOT role=dialog. Shows on hover and keyboard focus. Hides on Escape, mouse out, blur. Key APG distinction: tooltip only describes — it cannot contain interactive elements. For actionable popups use Dialog or Popover (no standard ARIA role yet for popover, use role=dialog or region).

title: Tooltips in the Time of WCAG 2.1 – Sarah Higley
insights: WCAG 2.1 SC 1.4.13: Content on hover or focus. Tooltip content must be (1) dismissable without moving pointer (Escape), (2) hoverable (pointer can move over tooltip without it disappearing), (3) persistent (stays visible). Most tooltip implementations fail (2). Article explains the 'safe triangle' cursor path technique for hover-on-tooltip. Critical for floating-ui/Radix tooltip implementations.

title: Component Composition Pattern – radix-ui/primitives DeepWiki
insights: Auto-generated wiki from Radix source code. Explains how compound components use React context internally: Root creates context, sub-components consume it. Shows the pattern for creating custom compound component primitives following Radix conventions. Context is named after the component to enable detection of incorrect nesting.

title: Navigation Landmark Example – W3C APG
insights: Implementation example: multiple navigation regions on same page each need unique aria-label. 'Main navigation', 'Site map', 'Breadcrumb navigation'. Without unique labels, screen readers cannot distinguish between them. aria-labelledby is preferred if a visible heading identifies the region.

title: Switch Pattern – W3C APG
insights: role=switch. aria-checked: 'true' (on) or 'false' (off). Different from checkbox: switch represents on/off binary state with immediate effect (e.g., enable dark mode), while checkbox is form input for list selection. Space key toggles. Focus handled as standard button. Accessible name required via aria-label, aria-labelledby, or visible label.

title: useDroppableCollection – React Aria
insights: React Aria's drag-and-drop primitive. Handles pointer drag, touch drag, and keyboard-accessible drag with Insert key. Screen reader virtual drag via drag-and-drop announcements. ARIA patterns: draggable items have aria-grabbed (deprecated but still needed for AT), drop targets announced via aria-live. Keyboard alternative: arrow keys to navigate, Enter to confirm drop. Fulfills WCAG 2.2 SC 2.5.7 Dragging Movements.

title: Data Grid Examples – W3C APG
insights: Three datagrid examples: basic, sortable, scrollable. role=grid, role=rowgroup, role=row, role=columnheader, role=rowheader, role=gridcell. Sortable columns: aria-sort='ascending'/'descending'/'none' on columnheader. Edit mode: Enter activates cell editor, Escape cancels, F2 alternative. Demonstrates aria-rowcount, aria-rowindex, aria-colcount, aria-colindex for virtualized/scrollable grids.

title: ARIA Examples and Patterns – w3c/aria-practices DeepWiki
insights: Auto-generated index of all APG patterns with code-level analysis. Maps patterns to their example implementations and test cases. Useful for identifying which patterns have AT testing coverage and which are documented with working code only.

title: Authoring Practices Guide (APG) Examples and Rules in 2025 – Elementor
insights: Accessible overview of APG for non-specialists. Explains 60+ examples for 30+ pattern types. Useful onboarding resource for teams new to WAI-ARIA. Highlights that APG is guidance (not normative spec) and implementations must still test with real AT.

title: Building Accessible Components with Downshift – LogRocket
insights: Tutorial showing useCombobox and useSelect in practice. Demonstrates how prop-getter pattern requires explicitly spreading ALL returned getters onto correct DOM elements — missing one breaks ARIA. Shows state reducer pattern for custom behavior override. Typeahead implementation using highlightedIndex and items array.

title: usePress.ts – React Aria Source
insights: Source implementation of React Aria's unified press handler. Shows: (1) pointerdown for mouse/touch start, (2) prevents click if pointer moved off target, (3) keyboard Enter/Space as press equivalents, (4) prevents double-firing from pointer+mouse event combos on touch devices, (5) isPressed state for visual feedback, (6) long press timeout via global pointer capture. Essential reading for understanding cross-input press semantics.

title: useCalendar – React Aria
insights: Calendar primitive using role=grid for the month display. Each day is role=gridcell with button inside. Arrow keys navigate days. Page Up/Down change month. Shift+Page Up/Down change year. Alt+Page Up/Down change decade. Home/End go to first/last day of month. Handles RTL layouts for Hebrew/Arabic. Works with CalendarDate, CalendarDateTime, ZonedDateTime types from @internationalized/date.

title: Window Splitter Pattern – W3C APG
insights: role=separator with aria-orientation, aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, aria-valuetext. Arrow keys resize panels. Home/End jump to minimum/maximum. Double-click or Enter toggles collapsed state. Focus on separator element itself. Rarely implemented but needed for resizable panel layouts (code editors, dashboards).

title: useGridList – React Aria
insights: role=grid for lists that contain interactive items (buttons, checkboxes, links within each row). Each row is role=row, each item is role=gridcell. useGridListItem enables focus management within rows. Different from useListBox: listbox items cannot have interactive content — gridlist rows can contain rich interactive content. Arrow keys navigate rows. Tab navigates within a row.

title: First Public Working Draft: WAI-ARIA 1.3 – W3C News
insights: W3C announcement of WAI-ARIA 1.3 FPWD (January 23, 2024). New roles and attributes added based on real-world AT usage patterns. Open for public comment period. Expected finalization timeline not yet set. Primitive library authors should monitor this for upcoming breaking changes in AT behavior expectations.

title: New Features in ARIA 1.3 – Orange Digital Accessibility
insights: Developer-focused breakdown of ARIA 1.3 changes. aria-description: distinct from aria-label (short name) — allows a longer prose description accessible only to AT. Example: tooltip text can be aria-description, while visible label is aria-label. role=mark for highlighted search results. role=suggestion for inline editing suggestions (Google Docs style). role=comment for annotation threads.

title: chakra-ui/ark DeepWiki
insights: Auto-generated architecture analysis of Ark UI. Shows how Zag state machines map to component APIs across frameworks. Each component: machine context = shared state, machine state = current behavioral state, machine transitions = what triggers state changes. Framework binding converts machine API to React hooks / Vue composables / Svelte stores.
